questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "If she had left earlier, ___"
questions.answer: she wouldn't have missed the train.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "If they had trained harder, ___"
questions.answer: we would have won the match.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "If I had known you were coming, ___"
questions.answer: I would have cooked dinner.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "If he had prepared better, ___"
questions.answer: he could have got the job.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "If we hadn't taken a wrong turn, ___"
questions.answer: we would have arrived on time.

questions.prompt: If I _____ (study) harder, I would have passed the exam.
questions.answer: had studied

questions.prompt: She would have got the job if she _____ (arrive) on time.
questions.answer: had arrived

questions.prompt: If they _____ (not take) the wrong road, they would have arrived earlier.
questions.answer: hadn't taken

questions.prompt: He _____ (not lose) his wallet if he had been more careful.
questions.answer: wouldn't have lost

questions.prompt: If we _____ (leave) at 7, we would have caught the flight.
questions.answer: had left

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "If I would have studied, I would have passed."
questions.answer: No 'would have' in the if-clause. Use past perfect: 'If I had studied'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "She would have come if she knew about the party."
questions.answer: Third conditional needs past perfect in the if-clause: 'if she had known'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "If he had worked harder, he would passed the exam."
questions.answer: After 'would' in third conditional: 'would have passed'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "We wouldn't have been late if we had leaved on time."
questions.answer: 'Leaved' is wrong. The past participle of 'leave' is 'left'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "If they had listened, they would have understanded."
questions.answer: 'Understanded' is not a word. The past participle is 'understood'
If I had studied, I would have passed.
She would have arrived if she had left earlier.
If it hadn't rained, we would have had a picnic.
If we had used the map, we wouldn't have got lost.
If he had trained harder, he could have won.
